# The Harbour Keeper

Chapter 1 — The Fog

Mara Voss watched the harbour fog swallow the last lamp on the pier. She had promised her brother Elias she would not return to Blackwater, yet here she was with salt on her boots and a letter she had not opened.

Elias had written that someone was living in the lighthouse again. Mara did not believe in ghosts, but she believed in debts.

Chapter 2 — Footsteps

The lighthouse stairs groaned under Mara's weight. Halfway up she heard footsteps above her — deliberate, unhurried, impossible.

When she reached the lamp room the chair was warm. A cup of tea sat untouched on the sill. On the wall, scratched into the paint: DON'T TRUST THE TIDE SCHEDULE.

Chapter 3 — The Schedule

The harbour master insisted the tide tables were correct. Mara knew they were wrong because Elias had died on a night the tables called calm.

She found the original ledger in the archive, pages torn out for the week of his death. Someone had replaced them with neat forgeries.

Chapter 4 — Blackout

The town lost power at midnight. Mara lit the old lamp by hand while the unknown occupant watched from the gallery.

"We need to talk about what you saw that night," the voice said. It was not Elias. It was the harbour keeper who had resigned the year before Mara left.

---

Unresolved thread: Who altered the tide schedules and why? The missing middle act should expose the harbour master's contract with the shipping company.
